title: Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum
taxonomy: Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyrtomidictyum, which is a group of ferns native to China and Southeast Asia. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse family of ferns, commonly known as wood ferns. The classification of Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum places it within the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns. Further classification includes its order Polypodiales, and the kingdom Plantae.

parts: Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum is a species of fern found in tropical Asia. intro: Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum, also known as Chinese Ostrich Fern or Chinese Lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cyrtomidictyum basipinnatum is often used to make herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the preparation of dried and powdered extracts.
